observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management reorganize the Inventory Committee and
consider designating its members who are not from any department with demanding
responsibilities and who will conduct a systematic and complete physical count of PPE
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 and ensure timely submission of
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide appropriate
information for the PPE items in the PPE schedules, such as project/item
specification, year constructed/procured, quantity, location of the project, and Office
to which the equipment/furniture and fixture are assigned.

We recommended that Management submits to the Audit Team the DVs and
appropriate documents to support the unrecorded disbursements amounting to
₱34,939,869.75 withdrawn against various funds of the LGU. (See Annex B for
details)

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strengthen accounting
controls over the Cash in Bank account, and effect necessary adjustments for valid
reconciling items, including the outstanding checks released from CYs 2007 to June
2024, which could be considered stale. (See Annex C for details)

We recommended that Management direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to
expense/charge the affected PPE accounts to Prior Period Adjustment and
to close in the books the corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts
in accordance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March
14, 2024;

b. Municipal Accountant to trace PPEs presented in lumpsum and determine
the acquisition cost of the equipment per item and prepare necessary
adjustments, if there are any; and

c. Municipal Treasurer to replace existing AREs with ICS for these items, if
any, except for low-valued semi-expendable property with expired
estimated useful life wherein the existing ARE must be automatically
canceled.

We recommended that Management require the:

a. Conduct physical count of inventories and to prepare and submit the duly
accomplished RPCI;

b. Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories for
consumption or distribution in the appropriate inventory accounts and the
consumption or distribution thereof as expenses upon receipt of SSMI of a
given period; and

c. Accounting Unit to maintain supplies ledger cards and the Local Treasury
Unit or the General Services Office to maintain stock cards as required in
both their offices and both Offices to conduct periodic reconciliation of
their records and make adjustments, when necessary, to reflect the proper
balance of the Inventory accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Serve demand letters to AOs with unliquidated CAs, not only to those still
connected with the LGU but also to those who are already retired, resigned
and transferred to other office/s, to immediately submit their liquidation
papers or settle their accountabilities with the LGU; and

b. Closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding CAs, stop the practice of
granting additional CAs to those who have unliquidated CAs and enforce
the issuance of certification that previous cash advance has been liquidated
and accounted for in the books as request under COA circular No. 2012-01
dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a. Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts;

b. Update appropriate details of the loan in the Notes to FS reflecting the
updated loan amortization, interest payment and GRT with the caption
that the interest payments are subject to changes; and

c. Coordinate with the bank management to reconcile and clarify issues
regarding the amortization of the loan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Review and analyze all the recorded obligations in the AP account for
current and prior years to ascertain their validity. After which, make
appropriate reversions/adjustments in the books for those payables that
are dormant and/or without valid claimants to ensure a fair presentation
of the AP in the FS; and

b. Closely monitor the status of the liabilities recorded in the AP account by
preparing a detailed schedule showing the necessary information, such as,
the record date, JEV No., payee, specific purpose, account used, and the
amounts of obligations, payments, and balances of each unpaid obligation.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials by adopting the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain and update the stock cards and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly;

Instruct all Inventory Custodians to ensure that all issuances of supplies and materials are covered by RIS and the Municipal Treasurer to use the RIS as basis for the preparation of the SSMI. Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the SSMI to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of the RIS which will be the basis of the latter to record in the books the expended portions of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Management commits to find solutions to this issue by designating a GSO that will be responsible in the procurement and monitoring of supplies and materials. The GSO will be preparing SSMI which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to recognize supplies and materials expenses.
observations.reason:
Due to the absence of an office that will discharge on a full-time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies, materials and property, all procured inventories received are recorded automatically as expenses.

Maintenance of stock cards and SLCs were not given priority.

Issuances of procured supplies and materials were supported by RIS. However, the preparation of the SSMI was not given priority.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of payable accounts to ensure that only valid payables are recorded in the books; and

Prepare a certified schedule of aging of dormant payable accounts for which there are no valid claimants/creditors and no supporting documents, thereafter, revert the verified amounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Government Equity Account by preparing the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify some accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Analysis and verification will be conducted with the Municipal Accountant in collaboration with the Municipal Treasurer, who is the former Municipal Accountant-Designate.

After analysis and verification, adjusting entries will be made if necessary to correct the accounts payable balances.
observations.reason:
Conducting periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of PYs payable accounts was not given priority.

Schedule of aging of payable accounts were prepared. However, verification of these accounts was not made, thus, no necessary journal entries were prepared.

Not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the pertinent transactions of the Intra-Agency accounts Due from/Due to Other Funds and reconcile the difference/s and prepare adjusting entries, as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will review the previous and current year’s entries, and will prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts.
observations.reason: Tracing and analysis of the Intra-agency accounts were not prioritized, thus, no adjusting entries to correct the balances was prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer immediately release the amount due to various barangays of the Municipality;

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain records and SL for each barangay to facilitate the release of RPT and CTC within the prescribed period; and

Require them to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the barangay shares from realty taxes stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code in the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will review the schedules of the shares of RPT due to barangays and will release the amount to barangays.

SLs will be maintained and updated by the Municipal Accountant for each barangay.
observations.reason:
In CY 2023, the balance of Due to LGUs – Barangays Share amounted to ₱1,652,350.01. This balance is an accumulation of the unclaimed barangay share.

Maintenance of SL per barangay was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to exert more effort in preparing and submitting the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We requested that such activities be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting.
observations.managementAction: Management will consider designation of additional staff to the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Year-end financial reports were submitted within the prescribed period, however, the monthly and quarterly were still prepared and submitted late. Consequently, the JEV, RCIs and other monthly transaction documents were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to exert extra efforts in the retrieval of land titles and other necessary documents as basis to establish ownership and determine proper valuation;

Allocate adequate budget for the titling of the subject land properties in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and reduce the risk of third-party claims; and

Conduct proper evaluation of the 13 RPUs to facilitate the immediate recognition as assets of the Municipality considering the criteria provided under IPSAS No. 17.
observations.managementAction:
Management will exert more effort in the titling of lots to ensure the Municipality’s ownership over these lots.

Approval of the Annual Budget is the top priority of Management.

Analysis and verification will be conducted by the Municipal Accountant on the documents of lots and will prepare necessary entries to correct the land account.
observations.reason:
Titling of some lots will be processed in CY 2024. Titling of other lots are not necessarily possible due to its timberland or foreshore classification.

Allocating adequate budget for titling is not possible due to absence of the approved annual budget for CY 2024.

Evaluation of lots were not given priority, thus, lots were still not recognized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee take full opportunity to conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE balances under COA Circular No. 2020-006 by undertaking the following steps:
Ensure the proper conduct of physical count of all PPEs and proper and accurate preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and reconcile the same with the property and accounting records;
Identify and recognize in the books the PPE items found at station; and
Determine non-existing/missing and unserviceable properties and follow the prescribed procedures for disposition and derecognition in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain an updated Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts.
We further recommended Management to consider designation of a General Services Officer (GSO), who will discharge on a full-time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies and property, maintain updated Property Cards per category on all Agency’s assets as recorded in the list of PPEs of accounting records, and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant examine all the entries made for the fund transfers, specifically those that were fully implemented but still outstanding and those unverified balances without any detailed documentation. This examination must include review of the previous years’ records and effecting correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances to be presented in the FS.
In case that the outstanding balances in the Due to NGAs account are determined to be funds for the completed projects and the remaining balances are no longer needed, we recommended that Management remit them back to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management require the concerned officers to submit the MOA or NADAI of the existing fund transfers to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Property Officer and the Municipal Assessor to exert effort in the retrieval of land titles and other necessary documents as basis to establish ownership and determine proper valuation; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify the recorded and unrecorded Land items to properly account them using the cost or fair value and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Land account balance in accordance with IPSAS No. 17 or any other applicable accounting standards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strengthen accounting controls over the Cash in Bank account;
Fast-track the retrieval of the appropriate documents to support the reconciling items presented in the submitted BRS and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the FS; and
Evaluate the status of the outstanding checks, especially those released from CYs 2007 to 2022 that could be considered stale.
Require Municipal Treasurer to update religiously his cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the BRS to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund or special accounts classification; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the MNGAS for LGUs on the proper recording and reporting of transactions, which requires that all regular purchases of supplies must be booked through the inventory account and the SSMI is needed as a basis for recording the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the SLC and stock cards for each item of supplies and materials, to monitor and control the status of the stocks on hand; and
Consider designation of a GSO that will discharge on a full-time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies, materials and property; maintain stock cards per category on all inventories as recorded in the list of inventory accounting records; and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the 471 disbursement vouchers from November to December 2023 and the 327 lacking disbursement vouchers from January to October complete with supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor;
Evaluate the process of preparing the disbursement vouchers and necessary documents and consider additional personnel for the Accounting Office with proper accounting background/training to prevent further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding years; and
Be reminded that the processing of payment for the disbursement vouchers must only be undertaken when they were properly approved and with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the personnel concerned to submit the lacking documents supporting the payment for the 15 percent mobilization fee, including the request letter from the contractors for the release of the mobilization fee and the irrevocable standby letter of credit/security bond/bank guarantee. We also recommended Management ensure proper documentation of future disbursements of the same type.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61, as amended by COA Circular No. 92-61A dated April 2, 1992, such as to:
Require all drivers to accomplish the driver’s trip tickets with all information required and ensure that they are duly signed by the authorized signatories and passenger/s after each trip and prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle summarizing all trips for the month;
Ensure that every transaction for fuel be supported by the duly accomplished and approved Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) or equivalent as the basis for the procurement/withdrawal of gasoline and diesel from the service stations; and
Prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption report showing among others, the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the Agency during the month, total distance traveled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the remaining unsubmitted disbursements vouchers and supporting documents for fuel amounting in total to ₱2,603,988.26 for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Council through the MDRRM Officer to:
Revisit the guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the MDRRMF, refrain from charging non-priority programs in the MDRRMF and ensure that the MDRRMFIP does not include administrative expenses instead prioritize appropriation for the procurement of equipment useful for disaster prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery;
Ensure that the PPAs to be charged against the STF-DRRMF are priority programs, such as but not limited to pre-disaster preparedness and post disaster activities, and be implemented at the earlies possible time; and
Assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the MDRR Office (MDRRMO) and to prioritize, in his preparation of the MDRRMFIP for CY 2024, the procurement of early warning systems, search rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage that are useful for the disaster preparedness and response.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of the STF-DRRMF amounting to ₱803,417.43 to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GFPS to:
Ensure that the essential elements in GAD planning and budgeting are in place such as capacity development on gender mainstreaming, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis and gender assessment and development and utilization of GAD tools; and
Utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on RA No. 9710 Magna Carta for Women, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 and other relevant international commitments.

We recommended that Management:
Limit the allocation and disbursement of funds for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses within the rates prescribed in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160;
Strengthen control on the disbursements of funds and refrain from paying expenses that are not properly documented; and
Exercise prudence in the utilization of funds. Ensure that allocation for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses are used only for the purpose for which these were established. Refrain from charging to this account expenses which must be covered by separate appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-MDRRM Officer and the Human Resource Management (HRM) Officer to initiate the process of designation additional personnel to complete the mandatory composition of the LDRRMO through any options provided in Section 5.5 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.
We also recommended that the Local Sanggunian prioritize and fast-track the approval of the 2024 Annual Budget to facilitate the creation of permanent positions in the MDRRMO and the existing personnel under job order basis will be given priority in hiring subject to existing civil service rules and regulations. This is to eliminate the risk of high personnel turnover and to protect the human resource investments of the LGU in sending these personnel to trainings and seminars.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Strictly enforce the submission of report on information and publicity of LGU PPAs as provided in COA Circular No.  2013-004; and
Direct proper coordination and reconciliation of the concerned LGU Officials, particularly the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant, in order to facilitate the preparation and submission of the required reports to the COA within due date.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review.
We also directed the attention of Management to Section 4.0, the Penalty Clause, of COA Circular No. 2009-001, which states that the inability of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements of the same must be subject to administrative disciplinary action and the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents may be suspended in audit.

observations.managementAction:
Management will review the composition of the Inventory Committee and will designate a GSO that will initiate the complete and appropriate conduct of inventory-taking and one-time cleansing of PPEs. 

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will prepare and update necessary supporting documents.

The Municipal Treasurer, who is the former designated Municipal Accountant, will assist on the retrieval of relevant documents and the same will be used to draw necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason:
An Inventory Committee was reconstituted, however, the preparation of PIP was not given priority.


The Inventory Committee was composed of Department Heads with demanding responsibilities, as such, the conduct of a complete physical count was not made and no reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.

According to the former Municipal Accountant-Designate, tracing of details on the PYs transactions is difficult.

Computation of Depreciation Expenses for PPEs procured in the PYs was not made possible due to absence of the complete details.

observations.managementAction:
Management promised stricter policy in the grant of cash advances.

Stricter policy on cash advances will be implemented.


Stricter policy on cash advances will be implemented.


Stricter policy on cash advances will be implemented.
observations.reason:
Demand letters were sent to accountable officers with outstanding cash advances, however, no liquidation was made.

Cash advances are still granted even if the previous cash advances are still outstanding.


Some transactions were not supported with complete documentation.

Instituting legal actions/sanctions to those accountable officers with cash advances were not given priority.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available for references. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Section 181(b) and (c) of GAAM, Volume 1.

We recommended that Management issue a demand letter to the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit his outstanding accountability amounting to ₱389,216.10.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will update religiously his cashbooks, and the same will be reconciled with the SL of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledgers per accountable officer are maintained by the Municipal Accountant. The cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer was not religiously updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN;

Direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the total road network in the Notes to the FS in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer will review the list of all infrastructure assets of the Municipality and will be provided to the Inventory Committee with complete description.

Management will designate a GSO who will initiate the submission of the required reports to certified by the Inventory Committee.

Monitoring cards for infrastructure assets will be maintained by the GSO to be designated.
observations.reason:
Listing of infrastructure assets, with complete description and cost segregation of road components, was not given priority.

Preparation and submission of the required reports were not given priority.


SLs were prepared but the LRNLCs and LRNPCs were not maintained and prepared.

observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO will prioritize the submission of the Utilization Report within the prescribed period.

The MDRRMO will ensure that the PPAs enumerated in the four thematic areas of the JMC will be prioritized.
observations.reason:
Not submitted within the prescribed period. The distance of the LGU to the Office of the Audit Team and the difficulty in the travel added to the delayed submission.



LDRRMF were still appropriated for some administrative expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the aforecited DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1, as follows:

Maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the programs, projects and activities within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all;  

Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the program, project and activity, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and

Adopt strategies to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of reported accomplishments to guarantee to the stakeholders the accuracy and reliability of information that are relevant in decision-making.

Also, we recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is solely used for development projects and no amount is transferred to other funds.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Office to:

Align its inventory procedures with existing rules and regulations; 

Submit the related requisition/issue slips and duly accomplished supporting documents to the Municipal Treasurer and Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate evaluation, recording and posting of receipts and issuances/utilization of COVID-19 vaccines in the books and property records of the Municipality; and

Continue spearheading the Municipality’s vaccination roll-out and ensure compliance with the vaccination program guidelines as a way of providing protection to its community from the adverse effects of the virus.
observations.managementAction: Management will designate a GSO that will be responsible for the monitoring of supplies. Management ensured compliance with the existing rules and procedures on inventories.
observations.reason:
Prescribed inventory procedures were still not followed.

Submission of the related requisition and issue slips was not given priority.

observations.managementAction: Upon approval of the 2024 Annual Budget, the Municipality will implement PPAs to address the issues in the SWM, including the provision of assistance to barangays in the establishment of MRFs and others areas.
observations.reason:
Only a few numbers of barangays have established MRFs.

Partial implementation only. Assistance for the restoration of not functional MRFs were not given full priority. Necessary training for all SWM personnel was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue demand letters to the beneficiaries to enforce collection; coordinate/ask assistance from other government offices, for instance COMELEC for the current registered locations of the debtors; and to take necessary steps to address the lacking documentation and follow-up collections.
observations.managementAction: Despite intense effort by the Treasury to locate debtors, it was not able to find the addresses, thus hinders issuance of demand letters. The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with COMELEC and Local Civil Registry but data privacy hindered release of information.
observations.reason: Absence of information hinders issuance of demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
Strict compliance with Section 51, Volume I of the NGAS Manual and other related guidelines for the proper recording of inventoriable supplies. 

Supply Officer maintain supply ledger cards and stock card; submit to the Accounting Unit on a weekly basis the SSMI as basis for taking up the expense; and conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials on hand and reconcile the same with the accounting records as often as necessary. 

All procurement of supplies and materials must be accounted for as asset, must be kept at the stock room of the Supply Officer for the meantime and issuance of such must be in accordance with the prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
All insurable properties be insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in the ensuing year and onwards as well as see to it that monitoring measures are in place in the renewal before its due date.

Ensure that funds are allocated to cover the premiums for the insurance so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated for any damage or losses arising from the risks the properties were insured for.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.
observations.reason: No funds were allocated for the payment of insurance.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Designated MDRRM Officer, in close coordination with the MDRRMC, to prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to coincide with the budget preparation of the LGU, as basis for the LDRRM budget to be incorporated in the Annual Budget of the LGU.

Likewise, in the LDRRMFIP, include under a separate caption the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years which must be implemented as planned in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and to avoid future reversion of the fund to the General fund.  Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 is required.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
The Offices of the three Municipal Economic Enterprises provide all the pertinent records of San Pascual Water System, San Pascual Local Electric Facility consumers/members and San Pascual Polytechnic College students to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the latter to set up the receivable accounts and record its corresponding subsidiary ledgers in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government. 

Close coordination of the aforementioned Offices is further recommended to evaluate the appropriate amount of receivables from the consumers and students to be recorded in the books of the LGU in order to reflect the accurate balances of receivables and income accounts.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.
observations.reason: Still no receivable accounts has been recorded from the consumers and students.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the Inventory Committee, in cooperation with all offices concerned, verify the
existence of unspecified and unidentified PPE totaling P177,617,457.01. Revisit
all available records to account for these PPEs including its custody. Indicate in
the remarks portion of RPCPPE the actual condition of these assets after
sufficient validation of its existence.

b. the GSO to prepare and maintain property cards for all class of PPE, including
land, land improvements, buildings, structure and infrastructure assets and
ensure that these assets be validated during PPE physical count.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. make the necessary entries for prior year adjustments for the
understatement of income totaling P19,941,164.44, understatement of PPE
by P997,156.33 and for its accumulated depreciation and understatement of
expenses by P3,191,376.58;

b. recognize income and expenses in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of
funds received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private
or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and
record entries relevant to the transactions; and

c. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial
Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to provide an
explanation on the unprogrammed disbursements of the Municipality. Update its
monitoring of Trust Liability-DRRM fund and ensure to review balances annually for
the preparation of LDRRMFIP and to avoid disbursements other than those
programmed for implementation.

Considering this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liability–
DRRM account per year of transfer as well as for those funds received from other
agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-
end in time for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal Accountant;

b. Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and proper liability
accounts the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and

c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during the
preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements such as stale
checks.

In consideration of the above observations, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. start maintaining complete and properly accomplished subsidiary ledgers for
every project of a source agency recorded as Due to NGAs and for every creditor
for specific purpose/fund recorded as liability of the Municipality;

b. reconcile the subsidiary ledgers and general ledgers; and

c. consistently update transactions and record all entries per subsidiary ledger in
order to come up with correct ending balances.

Considering this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. prepare and maintain a complete schedule of liabilities of the Municipality
indicating all pertinent and relevant information to support the balances reflected
in the ledgers and to enable to properly monitor funds which are due for return,
liquidation or payment and reversion to the Municipality’s funds; and

b. reconcile accounting records and effect the necessary adjusting entries for the
reclassification of P550,000.00 and be cautious in recording every transaction in
the Municipality’s books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to ensure that the
contract for garbage collection and disposal include all the items required under
COA Circular 2012-001 as well as stipulation for the sanction in case of delay or
breach of the contract. We also recommended the BAC to submit a detailed
computation for the rate of P999.00/cubic meter of waste indicated in the contracts.

Henceforth, we recommended that the MENRO institute controls on documented
monitoring of the daily trips of the garbage collector in accordance with the agreed
schedule and the correctness of the volume declared.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health
Officer to use the DPRI in setting the ABC or ceiling price of drugs and medicines
to be procured following the guidelines prescribed in DOH Department Order No.
2017-033.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of the
Sangguniang Bayan include among its top priorities the construction of a building
for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current
needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects; and

b. ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent Development Fund in the
succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned
and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to include in the
consolidated APP all infrastructure projects scheduled to be procured and
implemented for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the BAC to submit justification on the noted observation and submit
immediately all lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification
and audit.

b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the
supporting documents as required under Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 before processing payments to suppliers and contractors; and

c. Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality to identify and validate the existence of the recorded PPE and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
Thereafter, require the Municipal Treasury Office (MTO) and the Municipal Accounting Office to prioritize the reconciliation process and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted in the books accordingly;
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete and updated PPELCs to support GL account balances and the MTO to keep corresponding PCs for control purposes; and
Require the MTO/Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory and inspection of all unserviceable and obsolete items so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office concerned personnel trace back prior years’ acquisitions to have complete details of the PPE accounts that will serve as basis in the computation of depreciation, in order to present true value of the depreciable PPE in the FS.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant recompute the CY 2021 depreciation expenses for those PPE acquired prior to December 31, 2014 and make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts. The correct formula is as follows:

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories, and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain and update the stock cards and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended that Management:
Require all the concerned AOs to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately by demanding the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents. Otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of their salaries for non-compliance therewith;
Strengthen the control measures to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advances by strictly monitoring the period of liquidation and impose sanctions on AOs who are unable to liquidate on time; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant takes up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT and SET collected in CY 2021. Likewise, she must follow the correct accounting entries regarding collections of RPT and SET in order to reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET income, receivables and other affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Trace and locate the relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing the reconciling items and prepare adjustments, if any; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the discrepancy.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to exert extra efforts in the retrieval of land titles and other necessary documents as basis to establish ownership and determine proper valuation;
Allocate adequate budget for the titling of the subject land properties in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and reduce the risk of third party claims; and
Conduct proper evaluation of the 13 RPUs to facilitate the immediate recognition as assets of the Municipality considering the criteria provided under IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the remaining two plantilla positions for LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions set forth in RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 so that efforts will be coordinated in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building disaster-resilient local communities.

We recommended that Management update the 2012 RTC of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to consistently update its property records, particularly property cards to easily determine accountability and facilitate inventory taking. Further, we reiterate the recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all properties of the Municipality and ensure that all items are properly specified and identified detailing physical attributes and actual quantity on hand. Perform the reconciliation with that of accounting and investigate on the difference, if there is any. Lastly, keep track of the progress of the completion of the necessary documents for the evaluation and appraisal of all unserviceable PPEs for disposal.

In addition, require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required separate SAGF
for the Bayanihan Grant fund received from the national government.

We recommended that Management, through the GFPS:
1. Comply with the provisions of Section 3 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2016-01 by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD/HGDG Checklist
during the project identification and designing programs to determine
whether the same fully address the gender issues and concerns of the locality;
and
2. Maximize the implementation of the identified GAD projects to achieve the
objectives of the government regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the
communities.

We recommended that Management require strict adherence to Section 7.2.1(a)
of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001
governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts. Likewise,
direct the Municipal Accountant as well as the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal
Planning Development Coordinator-Designate to require the attachment of complete
supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to
infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the
supporting documents of all accumulated accounts payable as reflected in the
Schedule of Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2020 to back up the
authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the
Municipal Government;
b. In-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded
payables both for the current and prior year’s ledgers/files to determine
which are valid claims or fictitious accounts; and
c. The Office of the Municipal Accountant to take up the appropriate
reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for those established
transactions with no valid claimants and with dormant accounts in order to
have a fair presentation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive reinforce the manpower at the
Office of the Municipal Accountant to cope up with the required deliverables of the
Agency specifically on the observance of timelines in the submission of accounts/reports
to the Audit Team. In addition, instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly refrain
from allowing payment of claims without complete documentation and notify all
Municipal Officials/Employees for such directive for strict compliance.

Further, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to regularly and promptly
forward the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued AFs to the Office of the
Municipal Accountant on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to ensure
compliance with the required deadlines for appropriate recording in the Agency books
of accounts. After which, the same be submitted to the Auditor within the first 10 days
of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of
P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended strict compliance with Section 51, Volume I of the NGAS
Manual and other related guidelines for the proper recording of inventoriable supplies.
We further recommended that the Supply Officer maintain supply ledger cards and
stock card; submit to the Accounting Unit on a weekly basis the SSMI as basis for
taking up the expense; and conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials on
hand and reconcile the same with the accounting records as often as necessary.
Henceforth, all procurement of supplies and materials must be accounted for as asset,
must be kept at the stock room of the Supply Officer for the meantime and issuance of
such must be in accordance with the prescribed procedures.

We recommended that Management through the MSWDO:
(a) Submit the lacking documents; and
(b) Ensure that all documentary requirements are complied with before
release/distribution of SAP is made to avoid audit sanctions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive reinforce the manpower at the
Office of the Municipal Accountant to cope up with the required deliverables of the
Agency, specifically on the observance of timelines in the submission of
accounts/reports to the Audit Team. Meantime, it is suggested that the Municipal
Accountant maximize the existing workforce at the Accounting Section and introduce
measures to meet the required timelines.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF from the General
Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM. Ensure that the
unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE for each year be timely
transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up in order reflect the
correct balance of the cash and liability accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the respective amount
payable to the barangays of the Municipal Government as well as to the Province of
Masbate as mandated under the existing regulations.

Further, require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the
barangay shares from realty taxes as stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local
Government Code and shares from CTCs collections in the ensuing years.

We recommended that all insurable properties be insured with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS in the ensuing year and onwards as well as see to it that
monitoring measures are in place in the renewal before its due date. Ensure that funds
are allocated to cover the premiums for the insurance so that the Municipality will be
indemnified or compensated for any damage or losses arising from the risks the
properties were insured for.

We recommended that Management immediately remit or deposit the
withholding taxes and amounts due to different Agencies. Henceforth, periodic and
prompt remittances of these trust liabilities to agencies concerned must be imposed in
adherence to Section 69(1) of PD No. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and Inventory Report as well as the amount of PPE items which had no available documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of the differences.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer act on the matter accordingly, such as filing of legal action, if deemed necessary, and to gather sufficient information and documents to support the claim. observations.reason: Request for write off had not been forwarded to the Commission on Audit, given the management’s claim that they have exerted all efforts to collect.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office apply the correct categories for computation of business taxes for those entities and revise the rates of business tax. Also, Business Permits and Licensing Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales and maintain certified copies of income tax return to verify and validate the reported undercollection of P422,783.41 and demand collection/payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: Sample re-computation of business taxes paid by 18 business establishments showed that the LGU had applied correct computation of business taxes for those entities and the Municipal Revenue Code is revised in CY 2020 per Resolution No. 2020-03-054 series of 2020 approving Municipal Ordinance No. 9 otherwise known as the Revised Revenue Code of 2020 to arrive at more relevant and realistic rates in computing business taxes. Business permits applicants are required to submit proof of gross receipts/sales such as income tax and sworn statement of gross receipts/sales. Some application forms were not completely filled up and some supporting documents were not attached.
observations.reason: Upon sampled browsing of application forms for business permits, it was noted that there are some whose application forms are not completely filled up and are not supported with the required attachments. There are some whose certified copies of income tax return are not attached with the application forms. Validation of computed business taxes could not be performed due to absence of data on verified gross receipts for those entities.

observations.reason: Upon sampled browsing of application forms for business permits, it was noted that there are still some application forms whose required attachments were not attached and not completely filled up. Validation of computed business taxes could not be fully performed due to absence of data on verified gross receipts for some establishments.

observations.recommendation:

of complete physical count, reconcile inventory results with property and
accounting records.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to
the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and Inventory
Report as well as the amount of PPE items which had no available documents.

a. GSO to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and Report on Local
Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the
complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road
projects;
c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System as of the end of the year; and
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the
said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of
the differences noted to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC, Waterworks Division to
update the billing of all consumers, check and verify all delinquencies as of
December 30, 2019 and submit to the Municipal Accountant the detailed list for
recording of receivables. Likewise, we recommended to coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer for the monitoring of unpaid billings and strictly enforce the

to act on the matter accordingly, such filing of legal action, if deemed necessary,
and to gather sufficient information and documents to support the existence and
collection of the P2,900,000.00 receivables from Sta. Teresita, Inc. If collection is
still not possible after all remedies are exhausted, the management may request the
write off of the receivable to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office apply the correct categories for business
establishments as applicable and revise the computation of business taxes for
those entities with gross receipts exceeding the maximum of the range specified
in the Code or revise the Code in order to express the intention of the Municipal
Government to arrive at higher taxes; and
b. Business Permits and Licensing Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to
require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales and maintain
certified copies of income tax return so as to come-up with an accurate basis
for the computation of business taxes; and to verify, assess and validate the
reported undercollection of P422,783.41 and consequently demand the
collection/payment thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to see to it that the application
form for business permit are properly filled-up and completely supported by the
necessary documents prior to its endorsement to the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office (MTO); and
b. MTO to use the proper basis for the assessment of Mayor’s permit fees in

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to maintain a complete
database or list of business establishments operating in their jurisdiction from
which they can religiously monitor its compliance on the payment of necessary
taxes, permits and licenses prior to its operation;

business establishments under its jurisdiction by which they can monitor its
compliance to the payment of taxes and permit fees being imposed by the

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to declare the P67,169.45 tax collections free from protest and give due
notice to the taxpayers regarding the finality of the resolution. We also

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal

as of date and apply this practice prospectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
provide a backup computation of the project’s ABC for re-evaluation to justify the
cost, otherwise, the excess amount shall be disallowed in audit.

a) Augment manpower in MENRO to facilitate monitoring of actual waste
collection and implementation of projects/programs relevant to waste diversion
and reduction;

projects and make an action plan to track where the Municipality is currently at.
c) Instruct the MENRO to strictly implement issued ordinances on solid waste
management and impose the appropriate fines and penalties in violation
thereof.

We recommended that management and the Sanggunian coordinate in the efficient
enactment of the annual budget to ensure timely implementation of the priority
projects identified.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Development Council conduct proper
assessment and evaluation of the priority projects of each barangay and the whole
Municipality to avoid series of changes of the plan that affect the realization of the
Municipality’s goals set forth in their approved Local Development Investment
Program.

observations.reason: No reconciliation with the accounting records was made due to limited time and man power. See Audit Observation No. 1 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Appraisal Committee to appraise the 103 unrecorded parcels of land, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books of accounts said one hundred three (103) parcels of land using the appraised value, and make proper disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements on their actual usage as validated by the General Services Office; require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries for misclassification of accounts and overstatement due to properties not owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that lots acquired in 2014 for the barangays Gilerang Kawayan and Ilat North which were funded by both municipal and barangay funds be recorded in barangay books at the full acquisition cost.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and the report by the Municipal Assessor regarding the created Inventory Committee was agreed that the Appraisal Committee to appraise the 103 lots. JEV No. 100-19-12-3324 was prepared to recognize ownership of the 103 lots. Lots purchased for Gelerang Kawayan and Ilat North were noted Invoice Receipt of Property (IRP) No. 100-19-12-002 and 100-19-12-003 respectively and JEV No. 100-19-12-3504. The Municipal Accountant to make adjustments as per noted Invoice Receipt and JEV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant promptly record the completed projects funded by Trust Fund in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and the report by the Management agreed that the completed projects funded by trust funds are to be recorded as per JEV No. 100-19-10-2753.

observations.recommendation: We recommended transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (5-02-15-010) in the General Fund and the Department that unexpended balances from the General Fund be transferred to Trust Fund using the expense account “Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM to the Trust Fund” in the Trust Fund books before closing the same.
observations.managementAction: The payables which were outstanding for two years or more was made as per JEV No. 100-19-12-3503 and JEV No. 200-19-12-0364 for SEF. Transfer of unspent DRRM funds to the Trust Fund has been recognized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Administrator of Waterworks System to coordinate with Treasurer and strictly enforce the provisions of the 2011 Revised Revenue Code and recognize delinquent receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Administrator of Waterworks System to monitor the unpaid billings and strictly enforce the provisions of the revenue code; Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the List of Delinquent Waterworks System Fees with amount due and collectible and the latter to recognize in the books of accounts the accrued income and subsequently follow the accrual method of accounting in recording waterworks system fees.
observations.reason: Efforts to collect delinquents were implemented. Had already sent Notice of Delinquency to delinquent water consumers and currently conducting disconnection of water supplies to those who continuously being delinquent in their accounts. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office is still in the process of the preparation of the detailed list of waterworks delinquency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor pay/release the terminal leave benefits and revert the long outstanding payables immediately.
observations.managementAction: Reversion of payables which were outstanding for two years or more was made as per JEV No. 100-19-12-3503 and JEV No. 200-19-12-0364 for SEF. Terminal leave benefits for Vice Mayor Jay Fider and Councilor Efren Reyes were already paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor provide the uncollected monthly stall rental fees and goodwill and the latter be recognized as accrued income and subsequently follow the accrual method of accounting in recording monthly market rental fees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the officer in charge for market operations to strictly enforce collection for market monthly rental fees in the Market. The Sangguniang Bayan and the Market Vendor Association made revisions of some provisions of the Market Code to accommodate the stallholders’ request and suggestions to continue their business ventures in the market to avoid closure due to bankruptcy and to attract investors to fully occupy the vacant stalls. Collections are being enforced. They already issued demand letters to delinquent stallholders of San Pascual Public Market.
observations.reason: Monthly rental outstanding was P353,886.00 and monthly rental goodwill P3,315,000.00 still found outstanding as per latest report year end 2019. Most of the collections are being enforced; however, full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Development Planning and Management Officer ensure the timely implementation of projects and that the Local Development Council adhere to the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Local Development Council had adhered to the Municipal rules and regulations pertaining to the planning and selection of projects which are compliant to the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1. The barangays concerned pledged to coordinate with Municipal Engineer for work programs to be prepared and expedite the possible means for the said unimplemented projects to materialize. The LCE respects barangay decisions and reversion/realignment were made with corresponding barangay resolutions.
observations.reason: Delay in budget enactment, election ban, change in administration and reconstitution of MDC members affected timeliness. Some projects completed, some for bidding/appraisal and some unknown status.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated MENRO to prioritize the required permits and clearances and enjoin the Municipal Engineer to prepare the budget proposal for the construction of the lacking structures/facilities of the sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO indicated limited technical know-how and limited funds. The MENRO and Municipal Engineer are coordinating and the Municipal Engineer requested extensions to submit backup computations for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill. Some temporary measures and commitments to appropriate funds and continue efforts to implement lacking structures were stated.
observations.reason: Required funds and technical specifications were not fully prioritized or available; the MENRO and Engineer are still coordinating and compiling necessary documentation; full implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that projects charged out of the 20% Development Fund be specifically described and the Sangguniang Bayan approve such projects before disbursement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and Management agreed that the Municipal Development Planning and Management Officer to refrain from utilizing the fund for projects that do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. The LCE and MDC adhered to rules and the MDC identified projects compliant to DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Some projects stalled due to election ban and changes in administration; reversion and realignment occurred; further monitoring and adherence to planning procedures required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the MDRRMCC prepare the annual LDRRMO Plan and budget specifying programs, projects and activities intended for the unexpended balances of the calamity funds held in the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMCC and LDRRMO had the annual plans and budget specifying programs and activities and included these in the list of programs for CY 2020 submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to update its master list indicating the economic status of each senior citizen and select beneficiaries in order of priority, and conduct quarterly validation and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office updated the master list and priority of beneficiaries. Senior citizens 80 years old and above were given priority. Quarterly validation/monitoring to be conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal GAD Focal Person to perform gender analysis to define gender issues and design programs and activities to address them.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person continues to work on gender analysis and development programs. The Municipality will define gender issues concretely for effective programs.
observations.reason: Work on gender analysis and programs is ongoing; full implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Inventory Committee conduct inventory and inspection and embody findings in an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and follow rules pertaining to disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was created and conducted physical count of the PPE inside the municipal hall and outside the departments. Preparations for disposal of unserviceable properties are ongoing.
observations.reason: Disposal process pending as the Inventory Committee is still completing necessary documentation and procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of utilization report to substantiate actual use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Office stated that the financial assistance given to LIKAS has been submitted to the audit team for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor ensure job orders are hired based on actual human resource needs and stop acquiring services of a legal consultant except where allowed under the law.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO is now more diligent in hiring job order contractors to prevent duplication of permanent plantilla positions. The hiring and payment for legal consultant services has been stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to verify existing differences, review the existence of tax delinquencies from previous years, and conduct detailed reconciliation/comparison of beginning balances, set up, collection and any adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was done by the Accounting and Treasury that led to a lower discrepancy. Review of existence of tax delinquencies from previous years was conducted. Adjustments and reconciliation activities were performed.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the disparity is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality reconcile accounts at GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify differences, immediately remit the amounts due and identify causes of over/under remittances and make adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO and Accounting Department are performing monthly reconciliation to GSIS and have found that differences are due to timing differences in updating records; proper adjustments have been made.
observations.reason: Portions of discrepancies pertain to timing differences in update of records and supporting documents not available; adjustments have been made where possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended compliance with COA Circular 2013-004 on information and publicity of government programs/projects and proper documentation and computation for progress payments and retention.
observations.managementAction: Most of the observations relative to the noted contracts have been addressed; the Municipal Engineer and BAC ensured posting within required time. Notice of Award has been posted. Corrections and compliance are being implemented.
observations.reason: Some findings have been attended; however, full compliance and documentation for all contracts yet to be completed and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and the LCPC to identify programs, projects and activities of LCPC geared for promotion of rights and welfare of children and specify allocation of 1% IRA for the strengthening of LCPC.
observations.managementAction: Programs were identified and the Municipal Administration included PPAs that can be funded by 1% IRA and will inform the Sanggunian Bayan Members on PPAs that can be funded.
observations.reason: Action ongoing; further coordination and budgeting needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of justification relative to the noted contracts, the Municipal Treasurer to issue demand letters to debtors and coordinate with other government offices like COMELEC for assistance in locating debtors.
observations.managementAction: Most of the performance security observations were posted; the Municipal Treasurer coordinated with COMELEC for assistance but COMELEC could not provide information due to Data Privacy Act of 2012. Municipal Treasurer sought help from Local Civil Registry and other offices.
observations.reason: No full compliance yet due to limitations in obtaining debtor addresses and information because of privacy regulations; collection actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant correct all entries made and recover the irregular payment where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant sent a demand letter to the former official for the refund of P31,680.00 offset against the reimbursement and retain it as a disallowance per municipality’s records still due for collection from the former public official.
observations.reason: The concerned person/claimant does not fully concur with the amount; refund process ongoing and non-compliance on the refund of full amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Government comply with required signage formats and refrain from placing pictures, names and initials of officials on project tarpaulins/signboards as required.
observations.managementAction: Most signage requirements were being complied with; the Municipal Projects, Programs and Activities signboards were being posted as required and the municipality refrained from placing pictures and initials in excess.
observations.reason: Some requirements still not fully complied with for all projects; ongoing compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended construction of a permanent building for safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents as a priority.
observations.managementAction: A temporary storeroom was provided to the audit team and a new storeroom designated; plans to increase storage and construct a building for records preservation are among top priorities.
observations.reason: New storeroom provided but could not accommodate all files; construction of permanent building pending and prioritized.

Due to various assignments of the designated LDRRMO, the agency neglected to comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to the planning and utilization of DRRMF. Please see related Audit Observation  No.11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts. Direct the BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentations and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the Municipal Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.reason: The agency has poor compliance monitoring system on audit recommendations of the offered by the Audit Team  on deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the complete Assessment Roll and RPTAR to the OIC Municipal Accountant in order to facilitate the setting-up of RPT and SET receivables account. Likewise, prior year’s collectibles and collections thereto be kept updated through the maintenance of subsidiary ledger cards to ensure the reliability of the account balance of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables and the corresponding Deferred Income accounts.
observations.managementAction: Please see Audit Observation No. 2. (Restated Finding).
observations.reason: No coordination between the concerned departments of the LGU. In addition, lack of manpower at the Office of the Municipal contributed the non-maintenance of the necessary SLs for realty tax related transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management strict adherence to Sections, 7.8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts. Further, it was suggested that the Office of the Municipal Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.managementAction: They reasoned out that they find difficulties in its compliance due to the distance from mainland Masbate and mode of transportation to and from the municipality.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat has poor internal control on its record management and lack of coordination with the technical personnel resulting to incomplete/non compliance of the needed documentation of the PPAs implemented by the agency as well as depriving the Audit Team to conduct audit action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the persons liable to immediately settle the suspensions and disallowances or file an appeal memorandum with the COA Regional Director within the appeal period of six months from receipt of the Notice of Disallowance (ND).
observations.managementAction: Lack of accounting personnel to keep track of the compliance of required documentation.
observations.reason: Per information from the Acting Mun. Accountant, some parties manifested for its compliance. However, no actual/formal compliance of the required documentation for its settlement as of Dec. 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the created Inventory Committee,
conduct within 60 days upon creation, the physical count by account of PPEs and
reconcile results with the accounting records.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose in the Notes
to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and Inventory
Report as well as the amount of PPE items which had no available documents.

We recommended and the created Inventory Committee within 60 days upon creation, conduct sites inspection to update the actual usage of
each parcel of land reflected in the Tax Declarations, determine possible existence of
